text: ♪ "Ready Steady Go" by Harry Styles ♪

speaker: Craig
text: Good morning.

speaker: Craig
text: Thank you for joining us today at Apple Park and welcome to WWDC.

speaker: Stacey
text: We all look forward to this event and this week as we get to share our latest platform advancements and innovations with our developer community and with our users.

speaker: Shubham
text: WWDC also gives us the chance to celebrate our incredible global community of developers.

speaker: Shubham
text: The energy around Apple platforms has never been stronger.

speaker: Shubham
text: Developers are building more apps than ever with well over 1,000 submissions to the App Store every hour.

speaker: Tim
text: We're also investing in the next generation through 20 Apple Developer Academies around the world which have already helped tens of thousands of students begin their journeys.

speaker: Tim
text: And with WWDC, we hope to build on that momentum, inspiring even more incredible app experiences for our users.

speaker: Tim
text: At Apple, we've always believed that technology should be personal, powerful, and easy to use.

speaker: Tim
text: The tight integration of our hardware and our software unlocks the full potential of each of our products.

speaker: Tim
text: That same philosophy drives us to design our operating systems to work together seamlessly and to create a common foundation of technologies to help developers build great experiences across their apps.

speaker: Tim
text: We've seen that when we put powerful tools into people's hands, they can do extraordinary things from extraordinary places.

speaker: Tim
text: Places we only could have dreamed of. This is what WWDC is all about, introducing new technologies and innovations that push the limits of what's possible.

speaker: Tim
text: Today we're looking forward to sharing some exciting announcements including our latest advancements in Apple Intelligence and Siri.

speaker: Tim
text: With that, I'd like to hand it off to Craig to get us started.

speaker: Tim
text: ♪ "Clouds Will Carry Me To Sleep" by Gelli Haha ♪

speaker: Craig
text: Let's talk about our next set of releases where we have some exciting updates to share. This year, we put our focus into three areas.

speaker: Craig
text: First, improvements that make our platforms more responsive and easier to use in your day-to-day life.

speaker: Craig
text: Second, updates in trust and safety, where we're building on our commitment to provide a safe and trusted platform for kids.

speaker: Craig
text: And third, a big leap forward for Apple Intelligence, with an innovative architecture that unlocks a new Siri across platforms.

speaker: Craig
text: Along with many helpful features integrated into the apps you use every day.

speaker: Craig
text: This work applies to our new releases across all of our platforms, from iOS and iPadOS, to watchOS and tvOS, visionOS and macOS.

speaker: Craig
text: Speaking of macOS, for those of you that are WWDC regulars, you of course know that this is the moment

speaker: Craig
text: where I relay the latest exploits of Apple's crack marketing team, and their, shall we say, unconventional methods for naming macOS releases.

speaker: Craig
text: In this case, I'm afraid the story is incomplete.

speaker: Craig
text: Last I saw them, they just spilled out of their recently installed Apple Park "experiential ideation yurt" and piled into their microbus.

speaker: Craig
text: I tried to catch them, but they just handed me this note out the window before motoring northward. So here's all I've got.

speaker: Craig
text: ♪ "Beat of the Drum" by Homestead Wolfe ♪ "Dude, our chakra alignment has set our compass toward the summer of love.

speaker: Craig
text: But like, further. Our corporeal forms know no earthly tether.

speaker: Craig
text: We shall float on a span of gold over infinite seas, flying so high.

speaker: Craig
text: Marketing is such a great job." Okay, well, I'm afraid they haven't returned and I'm lacking the sensory amplifiers to crack this code.

speaker: Craig
text: So I guess the great era of macOS names must come to an end. Joz: It's Golden Gate, man.

speaker: Craig
text: ♪ "Beat of the Drum" by Homestead Wolfe ♪

speaker: Craig
text: [ Laughter ]

speaker: Craig
text: Oh thank goodness. Perfect. Our next version of macOS is macOS Golden Gate.

speaker: Craig
text: Alright, with that behind us, let's start with improvements that will make our platforms better than ever.

speaker: Craig
text: Our products are an integral part of daily life helping us at work, at school, at home, and everywhere in between.

speaker: Craig
text: So naturally we all have high expectations for them.

speaker: Craig
text: And we're always challenging ourselves to make our products ever more responsive, even more reliable, and that much more delightful to use.

speaker: Craig
text: So instead of just introducing a host of new features, we're also taking the features you already rely on and making them even better.

speaker: Craig
text: Because we believe the best operating systems aren't just built on big breakthroughs. They're built on sweating the details.

speaker: Craig
text: To tell you more, here's Stacey.

speaker: Stacey
text: ♪ "Of Course" by Yebba ♪ Stacey: Attention to detail has always been core to Apple's DNA.

speaker: Stacey
text: And this year, our teams doubled down on our relentless dedication to make the experience feel more polished and intuitive.

speaker: Stacey
text: We scoured every part of the OS for opportunities to refine our systems from the UI to the foundations.

speaker: Stacey
text: Nothing was off limits, no enhancement too small.

speaker: Stacey
text: We made things faster, smoother, even easier to use and we took care of a bunch of things you've been asking about.

speaker: Stacey
text: We're excited to share some highlights of this work with you.

speaker: Stacey
text: To kick things off, let's talk about one of the key themes of this effort: design. To tell you more, here's Shubham.

speaker: Shubham
text: Last year, we introduced our most ambitious cross-platform design update ever with Liquid Glass, which made apps and experiences even more expressive and delightful.

speaker: Shubham
text: Like with all major design updates, there's a natural process where we take a bold leap forward and then we continue to iterate.

speaker: Shubham
text: Part of how we do this is by listening to users and developers.

speaker: Shubham
text: Our team really appreciates your feedback and we considered it deeply as we refined the new design over the past year.

speaker: Shubham
text: Now, we're making some additional refinements starting with updating the foundations of how Liquid Glass is built.

speaker: Shubham
text: To ensure exceptional readability, we tuned Liquid Glass so it diffuses complex content behind it much more effectively, while also creating more depth and separation.

speaker: Shubham
text: While we think this is a great new default look, we also know that some users would like Liquid Glass to be even more clear, and others prefer a more tinted appearance.

speaker: Shubham
text: Since everyone's preference varies, we're adding a new slider and settings to adjust Liquid Glass, so you can set it anywhere from ultra clear to fully tinted.

speaker: Shubham
text: It's a whole new way to personalize your experience across apps.

speaker: Shubham
text: And for developers who have already adopted Liquid Glass, these customizations apply in your apps right away.

speaker: Shubham
text: In addition to improving Liquid Glass, we also wanted to reincorporate some of the cornerstones of macOS design that our users have always loved.

speaker: Shubham
text: For example, to help better distinguish larger groups of controls and text from the content beneath them, there is now a more uniform toolbar across the top of apps.

speaker: Shubham
text: This keeps text labels and headings legible while also providing greater structure.

speaker: Shubham
text: And to further refine the structure of apps on Mac, sidebars now expand to the very edges of the window. This helps reduce distractions along the left edge.

speaker: Shubham
text: And it delivers the same beautiful refractions as content scrolls underneath to create a more expansive feeling across your apps.

speaker: Shubham
text: Sidebar icons also regain their color, making it easier to distinguish apps and identify which window is active in the foreground.

speaker: Shubham
text: And every window on macOS now has the same tighter corner radius ensuring greater consistency across all of your apps, even if they haven't been updated.

speaker: Shubham
text: Finally, let's talk about app icons.

speaker: Shubham
text: Last year, we redesigned all of our app icons to establish a more harmonious look across apps and platforms.

speaker: Shubham
text: This year, we're taking this new design even further by integrating additional layers of Liquid Glass directly into the icon artwork itself.

speaker: Shubham
text: Not only does the refraction between these layers add character to each icon, but this new approach also makes icons appear sharper and more defined.

speaker: Shubham
text: They look great in your Dock, on your Home Screen, or when they're putting to an all-clear look.

speaker: Shubham
text: Together these updates culminate in an even more focused and approachable experience across your apps and across platforms.

speaker: Shubham
text: Now back to Stacey. Stacey: These design improvements are just the beginning.

speaker: Shubham
text: As we embarked on this journey to raise the bar for our products even higher, we also spent a lot of time digging into the fundamentals.

speaker: Shubham
text: Things like memory usage, CPU utilization, networking operations, display rendering, I could go on and on.

speaker: Shubham
text: But really the point is this: we optimized the parts of the system that make a big difference in the responsiveness of our products.

speaker: Shubham
text: For example, system animations are smoother, like swiping between Home Screen pages on your iPhone, entering Mission Control, or moving between spaces on Mac.

speaker: Shubham
text: Plus, a lot of things are faster. I'll just name a few. First, iPhone and iPad apps launch up to 30% faster.

speaker: Shubham
text: It's kind of unbelievable that it could get even faster, but the team found a way to preload the key data apps need when you open them, so they're ready to go.

speaker: Craig
text: This applies to third-party apps too. And content across the system loads faster.

speaker: Sumbul
text: For instance, after you shot some new photos, it used to take a beat for them to load in your library. Now, new photos appear up to 70% faster.

speaker: Shubham
text: And browsing files and transferring them from iPad to an external drive is up to five times faster, making it just as fast as the Finder on Mac.

speaker: Shubham
text: We're also doing something that will make older iPhones feel even more responsive, which has to do with our CPU scheduler.

speaker: Shubham
text: The CPU scheduler is a key system component that manages CPU resources across workloads as you use your iPhone throughout your day.

speaker: Shubham
text: Even when you're doing a ton of things at once, requiring a whole lot of compute. The CPU scheduler ensures the right work is executed at precisely the right time.

speaker: Sumbul
text: On our newer iPhones, iOS already has an advanced CPU scheduler.

speaker: Sumbul
text: This year we further optimized it so it's even more efficient when handling performance-intensive workloads.

speaker: Raja
text: And not only that, but we also figured out a way to bring it to older models, all the way back to iPhone 11.

speaker: Shubham
text: And yes, that does mean that iOS 27 is supported on iPhone 11 and all of the same iPhone models as iOS 26.

speaker: Shubham
text: This continues our industry-leading support and means iOS 27 is available to more users than any iOS release ever.

speaker: Shubham
text: There's also some work we've done that's about getting out of your way, clearing the path so you can work, communicate, and find the things you're looking for without speed bumps.

speaker: Craig
text: Like with network transitions, we're making it more seamless for your iPhone to transition between cellular and Wi-Fi networks.

speaker: Sumbul
text: We all know those moments where you find yourself jumping into Control Center to manually toggle off Wi-Fi to force a cellular connection.

speaker: Raja
text: Like when you walk past your favorite coffee shop with that extra strong Wi-Fi signal.

speaker: Raja
text: Or try to go online as you get off a flight and realize you're still connected to the airline's network.

speaker: Ann
text: To help you avoid moments like these, we've made your iPhone smarter about when to stay connected and when to switch.

speaker: Shubham
text: And for those times when you're in a low bandwidth situation, sending a big photo or video in Messages will no longer slow your conversation.

speaker: Shubham
text: You'll see a new send indicator for each message when things are taking a little longer to go through, giving you clarity on exactly what has and hasn't yet been delivered.

speaker: Craig
text: There's one last area I want to mention where we've made a big push to help you accomplish what you need with greater ease and reliability.

speaker: Sumbul
text: And that's Search.

speaker: Sumbul
text: We've all had that moment where you search for something you know is there, but it just won't show up.

speaker: Raja
text: So on iOS, iPadOS, and macOS, we've rebuilt the foundation of search that powers Spotlight, Photos, and Mail.

speaker: Raja
text: At its core is the search index, a rich catalog your device builds of all your content so it can understand what you have and where to find it.

speaker: Ann
text: We re-architected the index so it's more stable, more efficient, and more comprehensive of content both old and new.

speaker: Shubham
text: After you update, our new search infrastructure goes to work indexing the content of your device so it has a complete picture of what's already there.

speaker: Shubham
text: And as new content comes in, it's indexed almost immediately.

speaker: Craig
text: So when you go to search, you'll find what you're looking for Plus, in Mail, a completely new ranking system surfaces even more relevant results in your Top Hits.

speaker: Sumbul
text: So the email you're looking for is more likely to be the very first thing you see, even if it was sent months ago.

speaker: Raja
text: Those are just some highlights of the work we've done this year to make our products smoother and more responsive.

speaker: Raja
text: There are also some great features across your apps and products that we're excited to deliver, including some we've been hearing about from you!

speaker: Ann
text: Let me give you a handful of examples.

speaker: Ann
text: In Photos, your iCloud shared albums can now include all of the photos and videos from your latest adventures, because your friends on Android or Windows can join and contribute their own captures to the album.

speaker: Shubham
text: And what's more, Shared Albums now supports full-resolution sharing. In the Health app, Cycle Tracking now has support for perimenopause and menopause.

speaker: Sumbul
text: You can be notified when your cycle patterns are suggestive of perimenopause.

speaker: Sumbul
text: Log your related symptoms and read educational information so you can understand more about what's going on with your body and be better prepared to talk to your doctor.

speaker: Raja
text: On AirPods, we're delivering custom EQ so you can further personalize how your AirPods sound.

speaker: Ann
text: ♪ "Written Into Changes" by Avalon Emerson ♪ On Apple Vision Pro,

speaker: Ann
text: you can now turn panoramas you've shot into spatial scenes with incredible depth and realism, making your memories even more immersive.

speaker: Ann
text: And you can use those panoramas as your environment, surrounding yourself with a moment that is personal to you.

speaker: Craig
text: And lastly, over in Maps, we're giving Flyover an incredible boost.

speaker: Sumbul
text: Using aerial imagery combined with vision intelligence models, everything is rendered in stunning, sharp detail.

speaker: Sumbul
text: From beautiful architectural details to the shapes of individual trees, to the way light reflects off the glass of skyscrapers, you'll see cities around the world like never before.

speaker: Raja
text: We are thrilled about how these improvements touch so many parts of the experience across our platforms. Now, back to Craig.

speaker: Ann
text: With meaningful design improvements, enhancements that make things even more responsive, and some great new features,

speaker: Ann
text: we think the experience you have with your Apple products will be more delightful than ever. Now let's talk about Trust and safety.

speaker: Sumbul
text: We provide end-to-end encryption to help protect your conversations, strong privacy protections in Safari,

speaker: Sumbul
text: app permissions that keep you in control of your location, photos, and more, and life-saving features like Crash Detection.

speaker: Raja
text: Our long-standing commitment to safety is especially important when it comes to kids and teens who rely on our devices to stay in touch,

speaker: Ann
text: to explore creativity and enhance learning, and to build their independence as they grow.

speaker: Ann
text: And as parents ourselves, we're committed to building a safe and trusted platform for kids.

speaker: Ann
text: So this year we're taking a big step, expanding our child safety features with powerful and intuitive tools.

speaker: Ann
text: To tell you about our approach, here's Sumbul. Sumbul: Our work in this area is grounded in two core principles.

speaker: Sumbul
text: First, we recognize that every child is unique, and parents are in the best position to decide what works for their family.

speaker: Raja
text: Second, we believe in shaping Apple's child safety features based on expert health research.

speaker: Raja
text: The best available research from clinical and child development experts, as well as online safety experts, emphasizes the importance

speaker: Ann
text: of balancing learning, creativity, and connection with establishing boundaries around technology use.

speaker: Ann
text: This is critical in protecting adequate time for in-person social interaction, academics, physical activity, and sleep, all of which are essential for healthy child development.

speaker: Ann
text: The research also shows that kids under 18 using personal devices benefit from age-based protections guided by adult supervision.

speaker: Ann
text: And for those under the age of 13, access to personal devices should be limited and expanded when a child is ready.

speaker: Sumbul
text: As parents, we want our kids to develop healthy digital habits.

speaker: Raja
text: To help parents accomplish this, the American Academy of Pediatrics created the Family Media Plan.

speaker: Raja
text: And we're working with them to adapt it into a guide for parents that helps them establish those healthy digital habits using our child safety features.

speaker: Ann
text: We also continue to collaborate with researchers to understand the impact of technology on children's well-being and are committed to advancing the science in this area.

speaker: Ann
text: We know many families are looking for help to make sure their kids experience only what parents think they're ready for.

speaker: Ann
text: Here's Raja to show how they can get started. Raja: As parents, we want our kids to get started on the right foot.

speaker: Ann
text: So it's important to know that the first and most important step is to create a Child Account.

speaker: Craig
text: It immediately enables safeguards across the system, tailored to their age, like blocking adult websites, only allowing age-appropriate media, and setting age-based restrictions in the App Store.

speaker: Raja
text: A Child Account also provides access to powerful parental controls.

speaker: Ann
text: If your kids aren't on a Child Account today don't worry, you can convert their existing account.

speaker: Ann
text: Building off the child account, this year we focused on tackling the things parents are most concerned about,

speaker: Ann
text: like what content our kids can see, who they're allowed to talk to, when kids have access, and how parents can guide their kids' digital journey.

speaker: Ann
text: And we're providing parents with recommendations based on expert guidance to help them decide what's right for their kids.

speaker: Ann
text: Let's start with what content kids can see. On one hand, there's so many benefits to your child having their own device.

speaker: Craig
text: You rest easier since you can stay in touch, know where they are, and provide them with great apps that help them learn and grow.

speaker: Craig
text: But we also worry about opening the door to too much too quickly, and letting kids access things they're not ready for yet.

speaker: Ann
text: Starting this year, parents can ensure their kids start focused by giving them access to only the content they're comfortable with.

speaker: Ann
text: Then, gradually and when appropriate, add more over time.

speaker: Ann
text: It begins with an easy-to-use Setup Assistant, where parents can choose exactly what they want to allow.

speaker: Ann
text: A few essential apps, a recommended set, or the specific ones they want.

speaker: Ann
text: Over time, a child will be ready to add more, and parents can remain in control. To tell you more, here's Ann.

speaker: Craig
text: The best way to stay on top of what our kids can do is to manage which apps they download.

speaker: Craig
text: On the App Store, every app has an age rating that helps us parents decide if it's appropriate for our kids.

speaker: Craig
text: When a child finds an app they want, they need to ask for permission.

speaker: Craig
text: Parents then decide, with helpful context, if it's the right app for their child and review these requests right in Messages with Ask to Buy.

speaker: Ann
text: This year we're expanding what parents can approve beyond apps to the web with Ask to Browse.

speaker: Ann
text: Kids need to ask when they want to view a new website. Parents can take a closer look before approving.

speaker: Ann
text: This works seamlessly across iPhone, iPad, and Mac with Safari. Ask to Browse and Ask to Buy are both on by default for kids under 13.

speaker: Ann
text: Parents can also enable these settings for teens. How about who kids can talk to? Parents are in control here, too.

speaker: Craig
text: You can start off with just the immediate family, add grandparents, and as kids grow, they want to connect with new people.

speaker: Craig
text: Just like with apps, parents can have their kids ask for permission before connecting with anyone new.

speaker: Craig
text: As parents, we also want our kids' conversations to be safe, free from inappropriate content.

speaker: Ann
text: Today, if a child receives or attempts to send images or videos that might contain nudity, communication safety warns them and blurs the content.

speaker: Ann
text: It even works on live FaceTime calls.

speaker: Ann
text: This year, communication safety also intervenes before kids can see gore or violent content in shared images or videos.

speaker: Ann
text: Next, let's talk about managing when your child has access to the apps on their personal devices.

speaker: Craig
text: With Screen Time today, parents can see how much time a child is spending on their device.

speaker: Craig
text: At my house, it can be tough to get the kids to put down their favorite videos or games.

speaker: Craig
text: We know parents want to better manage the time their kids spend in certain apps.

speaker: Craig
text: So we're providing them with a flexible and easy new way to help them do this with Time Allowances.

speaker: Craig
text: Now, front and center, are Entertainment, Games, and Social Media, with a daily time allowance recommendation that spans all three, giving parents a helpful starting point.

speaker: Ann
text: Each of these categories also has its own time allowance recommendation to start with.

speaker: Ann
text: These are based on a child's age and developed with the best clinical and child development experts, including, as Sumbul mentioned, the American Academy of Pediatrics.

speaker: Craig
text: And we will actively work with them to update these recommendations as the science evolves and we get feedback from parents.

speaker: Craig
text: When it comes to Social Media, we know parents are concerned.

speaker: Craig
text: Experts recommend children under 13 don't use Social Media, and that parents carefully consider when their teen is ready.

speaker: Craig
text: And because every child is unique, parents are in control and can always adjust any of the suggested allowances based on what's best for their child.

speaker: Craig
text: We also know that every day isn't the same. A school day isn't the same as a weekend.

speaker: Ann
text: And when it comes to school, we believe devices in the classroom should be used to support learning.

speaker: Ann
text: So now, parents can easily set up a schedule and choose which apps are available to kids at different times of the day.

speaker: Ann
text: This helps kids stay focused when it matters, like during school.

speaker: Craig
text: And Schedules work hand in hand with Time Allowances, letting you do things like offer extra time to watch a movie on the weekend.

speaker: Craig
text: And of course, parents have the ability to fully customize these schedules to fit their family's routines.

speaker: Craig
text: Parents also want simple and intuitive ways to guide their kids' digital journey to be healthy and positive.

speaker: Craig
text: So we've completely redesigned Screen Time. At a glance, parents can see how their kids have been using their devices.

speaker: Craig
text: And in the moment, parents can quickly adjust access with just a tap. Now, what about the experiences kids have when exploring within apps?

speaker: Mike
text: What's possible today in apps feels almost limitless, from more ways to connect with others to AI and immersive gaming.

speaker: Ann
text: But as parents, we want to make sure our children are ready for these experiences.

speaker: Craig
text: While Apple's powerful controls help parents manage which apps their child can access and when

speaker: Craig
text: it's developers who play an important role in ensuring kids are getting age-appropriate experiences within apps.

speaker: Craig
text: Many apps already offer parental controls to help shield kids from content they shouldn't see.

speaker: Craig
text: We believe every app has that same responsibility. To help developers get started, we offer a full suite of APIs and resources.

speaker: Mike
text: For example, to help protect against nudity and violent content in apps or help ensure parents approve any new contacts in apps.

speaker: Mike
text: Developers can also use the Declared Age Range API.

speaker: Mike
text: This allows them, in a privacy-preserving way, to use a child's age range to tailor their app experience accordingly.

speaker: Craig
text: These and many more resources are available to help developers build safer experiences for kids.

speaker: Craig
text: And now back to Craig.

speaker: Craig
text: To sum it up, we're giving parents powerful, easy-to-use tools to help manage what kids can see, who they can talk to, and when they have access.

speaker: Craig
text: And for parents who want to learn more, we have a new website that highlights all of our child safety features and provides answers to common questions, including how to get started.

speaker: Mike
text: Together, this makes for a big update underscoring our ongoing commitment to building a safe and trusted platform for kids.

speaker: Mike
text: Now, let's turn to intelligence.

speaker: Mike
text: AI is incredibly powerful technology with the potential to shape society in profound ways, and, with proper care, unlock meaningful benefits for people everywhere.

speaker: Craig
text: Still, some appear to be racing forward, seemingly pursuing AI, for the sake of AI, without clear regard for the people, all of us, that it's ultimately meant to serve.

speaker: Craig
text: At Apple, our mission has always been to turn the potential of advanced technology into helpful and intuitive products for everyone.

speaker: Craig
text: This has been true since the dawn of personal computing, and it's never been more important than today.

speaker: Craig
text: We believe that truly helpful AI must be centered around you and your needs.

speaker: Mike
text: This means integrating AI deep into the products you use every day, grounding it in your personal context

speaker: Mike
text: and the apps you rely on, and of course designing it with privacy at every step. This is our vision for Apple Intelligence.

speaker: Mike
text: And today we're taking a big step forward with the next generation of Apple Intelligence that makes your Apple products more personal and useful.

speaker: Craig
text: We'll tell you about its bold new architecture, how it unlocks an entirely new Siri that's much more responsive to your needs, and how it makes your apps smarter and more useful.

speaker: Craig
text: So let's get started with our new architecture. It's centered around you and the Apple products that you use every day.

speaker: Craig
text: And at the heart of Apple Intelligence are Apple Foundation models.

speaker: Mike
text: This year, we embarked on a deep collaboration with Google, leveraging the technologies behind their Gemini family of models.

speaker: Mike
text: Together, we created the next generation of Apple Foundation models for our integrated Apple Intelligence experiences,

speaker: Mike
text: and adapted these new models to run on-device and on servers using Private Cloud Compute.

speaker: Mike
text: These powerful server and on-device models unlock a huge upgrade for Apple Intelligence with state-of-the-art understanding and reasoning

speaker: Mike
text: and multiple modalities, like powerful image understanding and generation.

speaker: Craig
text: So you can create realistic images, edit photos like never before, and get answers about visual content.

speaker: Craig
text: And we went further, optimizing for the most capable of our Apple silicon systems with a second, even more powerful version of our on-device model.

speaker: Mike
text: This model lets supported products understand and generate speech, along with understanding text and images.

speaker: Mike
text: And it delivers even higher accuracy for system-wide dictation, better natural language understanding, and incredibly expressive voices.

speaker: Mike
text: Next, we integrated our models deeply into our platforms, enabling a wide range of system-wide capabilities.

speaker: Mike
text: And Apple Intelligence securely coordinates across them with a new system orchestrator.

speaker: Mike
text: These capabilities start with personal context understanding.

speaker: Justin
text: Whether you're looking at a photo from years ago or a note you took recently, you can find what you're looking for just by asking.

speaker: Craig
text: Behind the scenes Apple Intelligence uses Spotlight and its powerful semantic index, which quickly surfaces what you need in the moment.

speaker: Craig
text: And it works with any app that integrates with it.

speaker: Mike
text: Plus, with broad world knowledge, Apple Intelligence can go out to the web to get up-to-date information,

speaker: Mike
text: then use Private Cloud Compute to generate an answer whether you're researching for school or brainstorming for a party.

speaker: Mike
text: And with App Actions, Apple Intelligence can draw on the app toolbox to find the right tools from your apps to complete your request.

speaker: Mike
text: Like when you're drafting an email from scratch or editing and sharing a set of photos.

speaker: Mike
text: Finally, with on-screen awareness, Apple Intelligence can tailor its assistance in the moment based on the app you're using and what you're doing.

speaker: Justin
text: This powerful new architecture unlocks a wide range of helpful experiences, from an all-new Siri to intelligent features across your most used apps.

speaker: Craig
text: And because they're available system-wide, they're even more useful for the things you do every day.

speaker: Mike
text: Of course, like everything we do at Apple, this architecture is built privacy-first.

speaker: Mike
text: Today, many AI providers talk about privacy, but by default, most of them retain your personal interactions,

speaker: Mike
text: leaving the onus on you to defend your privacy, like using temporary chats, deleting conversations, or even turning off entire features.

speaker: Mike
text: At Apple, we believe privacy in AI is non-negotiable.

speaker: Mike
text: Apple Intelligence uses on-device processing, as well as Private Cloud Compute, which ensures your data is not stored or accessible to Apple or anyone else.

speaker: Justin
text: Your data is only used to execute your request. And outside experts can continue to verify this promise at any time.

speaker: Justin
text: Now we're excited to show you all the ways that Apple Intelligence elevates your Apple products, starting with an all-new Siri.

speaker: Mike
text: To tell you more, here's Mike.

text: ♪ "you open my eyes" by Tennyson ♪

speaker: Mike
text: Siri is the personal assistant that helps you get things done just by asking.

text: And today we're introducing an entirely new version of Siri, unlocked by Apple Intelligence.

text: We call it Siri AI.

speaker: Justin
text: Siri AI uses our new Apple Intelligence capabilities, and you can tap into them in the same ways you access Siri today,

speaker: Justin
text: like by saying, "Hey Siri." This includes personal context understanding, app actions, on-screen awareness, image understanding, and access to broad world knowledge.

speaker: Mike
text: So Siri is now a profoundly more capable assistant that helps you find what you need and gets more done.

text: With Apple Intelligence, we're bringing your exciting new features across apps like Safari, Passwords, Messages, and more.

text: Let's start with Safari. Safari was designed to help you move fast and stay focused on what matters.

speaker: Beth
text: One of the ways we're delivering on that this year is a simpler, smarter way to manage your tabs. We all know how quickly a browsing session can lead to lots of open tabs.

text: Today, Safari provides helpful tools like Profiles and Tab Groups to manage your browsing. But it can still be a lot of work to keep track of everything.

speaker: Cecilia
text: Now, Safari can use Apple Intelligence to tame your tabs by organizing them into topics.

speaker: Cecilia
text: Safari analyzes each page, identifies similarities, and then brings related tabs together, making it easier to find what you're looking for.

speaker: Leslie
text: And Safari keeps you organized. It adds new related tabs to a topic even as you browse.

speaker: Beth
text: When you're done for the day, you can close an entire topic or save it for later as a Tab Group.

speaker: Beth
text: Safari also uses Apple Intelligence to help you stay on top of updates you care about.

speaker: Beth
text: Sometimes we keep tabs open because we're waiting for something, like camp signups or a product to come back in stock.

speaker: Beth
text: That can lead you to constantly refresh to see if something's been updated.

text: And later, when Safari detects the change, you'll get a notification so you can take action.

speaker: Alok
text: And so our goal for bringing AI into the Photos app is to help photographers enhance their images in ways that respect the original moment.

text: We have three features to help make your compositions frame-worthy.

text: First, the popular Clean Up tool is getting a big upgrade, so you can remove distractions with better quality and more realistic infill, even when the scene is complex like this.

speaker: Alok
text: Next, sometimes you wish you had a little more space around your subject, or you need a different aspect ratio for a project.

speaker: Alok
text: Now you can expand your images with the Extend tool to give your subjects more breathing room or to straighten a crooked horizon without cropping out anything important.

speaker: Alok
text: And we have one more super impressive new editing tool.

speaker: Alok
text: One that builds on Clean Up and Extend, and our deep understanding of spatial models, thanks to Apple Vision Pro.

speaker: Alok
text: Have you ever looked at a photo and wished you would have had more time to think about the composition when you captured it?

speaker: Alok
text: Maybe you accidentally framed a sign above someone's head or the photo would have been more symmetrical if you'd stepped to the right.

speaker: Alok
text: Or you missed eye contact with your subject by a fraction of a second.

speaker: Alok
text: Now, with Spatial Reframing, you can fix all these things after the photo was taken. Let me show you how it works.

speaker: Alok
text: Let's take a look at a picture I recently took of my kids.

speaker: Alok
text: I love this cute photo of my son and daughter celebrating their last day of school, but I wish I'd spent more time thinking about the framing.

speaker: Alok
text: When I tap Edit, you'll see a new Tools option in the lower right.

speaker: Alok
text: Here I'll find all the editing features that use our Apple Intelligence image models and Private Cloud Compute, including Spatial Reframing.

speaker: Alok
text: Let's tap Reframe. Once the image loads, you'll see that the UI is super simple. You can interact with the photo directly to adjust the framing.

speaker: Alok
text: I can touch, drag, and the perspective shifts as if I had repositioned the camera in the original scene.

speaker: Alok
text: This capability uses on-device spatial models so you can preview the effect in real time.

speaker: Alok
text: And as you adjust the photo, you'll see a blur around the edges of the original image, which will be filled in later by our generative models.

speaker: Alok
text: And I can pinch to zoom in closer, and there. That looks great.

speaker: Alok
text: Now I'll tap Reframe.

speaker: Alok
text: This feature is super cool, because it combines our advanced on-device spatial models with our powerful image generation model on Private Cloud Compute to create the final photo.

speaker: Alok
text: And even better, it only generates new content to fill in the gaps where the perspective has been shifted.

speaker: Alok
text: This ensures that the reframed photo stays consistent with the original scene. Here's the final result, and it looks great.

speaker: Alok
text: As I toggle before and after, you can see my kids are much better framed in the scene after moving the camera down, and the content that was filled in looks so natural.

speaker: Alok
text: It's like I was able to go back in time and adjust my camera in the moment I snapped the photo.

speaker: Alok
text: So those are the new Apple Intelligence capabilities in Photos.

speaker: Alok
text: And what's great is that these features work on almost any photo in your library, even older photos or photos taken with other cameras.

speaker: Alok
text: And now, back to Craig. Craig: Those are just some of the ways that Apple Intelligence is up-leveling so many of the apps you use every day.

speaker: Alok
text: These new features across apps will be coming to all our Apple Intelligence-supported languages.

speaker: Alok
text: And they'll be available for free with our latest software releases.

speaker: Alok
text: Some features, including image generation, have daily usage limits because they rely on powerful server models.

speaker: Alok
text: Increased access is available with most iCloud+ subscription plans, which also include Apple Intelligence support for compatible home cameras.

speaker: Alok
text: So that's our next generation of Apple Intelligence.

speaker: Alok
text: From an entirely new Siri that's more capable and conversational, to new ways for browsing the web, expressing your creativity, and editing your photos.

speaker: Alok
text: All with privacy built in. This is a big step forward on our journey to integrate powerful AI into the core of our platforms and make our products even more personal and useful.

speaker: Alok
text: And that's not all. We're bringing many more Apple Intelligence features to our users.

speaker: Alok
text: This update will be supported on the same product models that support Apple Intelligence today.

speaker: Alok
text: Developers can start trying out our new version of Siri today.

speaker: Alok
text: And for customers, we'll be launching Siri AI in beta later this year. We're excited to hear your feedback.

speaker: Alok
text: Siri AI will not be available initially in the EU on iOS and iPadOS.

speaker: Alok
text: We're working hard to find a path forward that preserves our users' privacy and security.

speaker: Alok
text: In China, Siri AI and the other new Apple Intelligence features will not be available while we work through regulatory requirements.

speaker: Alok
text: Next, for developers, there's a lot to be excited about, with new tools and technologies to help you build incredible apps.

speaker: Alok
text: Now you can bring Apple Intelligence right into your apps using technologies you already know, like App Intents,

speaker: Alok
text: to help users surface the right information and get things done just by asking Siri.

speaker: Alok
text: For example, because the messaging app Line indexes its content into Spotlight, users can ask Siri to help them find information from their conversations in the app.

speaker: Alok
text: Or because the calendar app, Structured, adopts app intents, a user can just ask Siri to create an event and quickly add it to their timeline, and it's done.

speaker: Alok
text: And so many of you are already building the next generation of intelligent apps - powerful, fast, and private using the on-device model through the Foundation Models framework.

speaker: Alok
text: You can now use images as input in addition to text.

speaker: Alok
text: Extend the model capabilities with custom skills, and even use models running on servers, all through the same Swift API.

speaker: Alok
text: For example, many people ask me, "How can you possibly do this many presentations and always be in some version of a blue shirt?" Answer: I apply advanced technology.

speaker: Alok
text: With an app like Daydream, I can select a photo from an event and the app can use the on-device model to identify every piece of my outfit.

speaker: Alok
text: Then it uses its deep fashion expertise to help me find something right within my narrow fashion comfort zone.

speaker: Alok
text: In addition to Apple Foundation models, you can now easily bring other models to run locally in your app with the full power of Apple silicon,

speaker: Alok
text: using the new Core AI framework available on all our platforms. That's apps and intelligence working together.

speaker: Alok
text: And it comes back to what has always made the apps you make so great.

speaker: Alok
text: Rich, native experiences now enhanced with intelligence, not replaced by it. Our app ecosystem has never been more vibrant.

speaker: Alok
text: More developers, more apps, more innovation than ever before.

speaker: Alok
text: Like with agentic coding, it's changing how apps get built, and Xcode is the best place to build them.

speaker: Alok
text: The coding assistant can now localize an entire app and interact with simulated devices. And you can extend its capabilities with custom skills.

speaker: Alok
text: You can also choose the model and agent of your choice, now including Gemini, and connect to tools like Figma and GitHub.

speaker: Alok
text: And we're reimagining how you can test apps with the all-new Device Hub that brings every device, simulated and real, into one unified interface.

speaker: Alok
text: You can now simulate multi-touch controls, like swipe and pinch, change your app's appearance in one click, and even resize it dynamically to iterate like never before.

speaker: Alok
text: You'll hear much more about our tools, languages, and frameworks in the Platforms State of the Union.

speaker: Alok
text: And we have a big week ahead with technical sessions, live forums, group labs with Apple engineers, and more.

speaker: Alok
text: It's going to be an amazing WWDC. Now, back to Tim.

speaker: Tim
text: Thank you, Craig, and thanks to all of our presenters.

speaker: Tim
text: We're excited to bring a wide range of improvements to each of our platforms, making them even more useful and delightful.

speaker: Tim
text: We're also continuing to provide more features that will help kids enjoy their Apple products to the fullest, while ensuring their experiences are most appropriate for them.

speaker: Tim
text: And the next generation of Apple Intelligence introduces new capabilities, including Siri AI,

speaker: Tim
text: that delivers rich conversations and draws upon your personal context to help you get more done.

speaker: Tim
text: This, along with our other new features and capabilities, will help users get even more out of our products.

speaker: Tim
text: Our new OS releases will be available as developer betas today, with a public beta coming next month.

speaker: Tim
text: And they will be available to users this fall.

speaker: Tim
text: On a personal note, some of the greatest highlights of my time as CEO have been events like this.

speaker: Tim
text: Sharing powerful new tools with all of you and then seeing what you create with them has been a constant reminder that imagination has no limits.

speaker: Tim
text: Over the years you have helped people connect, create, learn, and experience the world in extraordinary new ways.

speaker: Tim
text: And with the incredible capabilities we introduced today and so many more still to come, I truly believe the best is still ahead.

speaker: Tim
text: At Apple, creating the best products in the world to deliver experiences that enrich people's lives has always been our North Star.

speaker: Tim
text: It's been the honor of a lifetime to help advance that mission with teams whose creativity, care, and conviction continue to make a lasting difference in people's lives.

speaker: Tim
text: Thank you all for joining us. Let's have a great WWDC!
